page: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-48 is a general-purpose server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. It features 48 dedicated physical cores on the x86_64 architecture with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio, meaning hyperthreading is not utilized. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Cache specifications include 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or bundled GPU hardware.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core performance ranking in the top 10% of tested servers. The instance also demonstrates strong performance in Passmark CPU tests, software compilation, ray tracing, and image processing. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is strong at 5702.82 GB/sec, though large uncached block read and write speeds fall into the average tier. For language model workloads, the instance achieves top-tier prompt processing speeds on small models, but performance drops to the bottom 25% during text generation on large 70B models due to the lack of hardware acceleration.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the t2d-standard-48 offers high resource density and strong multi-threaded compute capabilities without the overhead of hyperthreading, making it a viable option for workloads requiring predictable core performance. The lack of bundled local storage and GPUs represents a tradeoff, focusing the instance's value on pure CPU and memory capacity. This profile is highly suitable for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, database operations, and medium-scale web serving.
description: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-48 is a general-purpose server instance configured with 48 dedicated physical cores on an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor and 192.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, it features a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio and lacks bundled local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core performance, particularly in stress-ng and small-model LLM prompt processing, alongside strong results in software compilation, database operations, and image processing. However, memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks remains average, and large-model text generation is weak. This server offers high resource density and predictable CPU performance, making it well-suited for multi-threaded workloads, software development pipelines, and database hosting.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-48 features 48 dedicated AMD EPYC physical cores and 192 GB RAM. It delivers top-tier multi-core performance for databases and software compilation.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-48 is a general-purpose instance with 48 AMD EPYC cores and 192 GB RAM, optimized for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A dedicated 48-core AMD EPYC instance delivering strong multi-threaded performance and balanced memory for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated AMD EPYC 7B13 physical cores with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio.
192.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core.
Top-tier multi-core performance in stress-ng and small-model LLM prompt processing.
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators.
Optimized for software compilation, database operations, and multi-threaded applications.
